openmeter/billing/charges/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing charges.Adapter and charges.ChargesSearchAdapter; provides all DB access for the charges domain (search, advancement candidates) with strict transaction rebinding via entutils.TransactingRepo on every exported method.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method
patterns.description: Every exported adapter method body wraps in entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx, tx *adapter) ...) so it rebinds to the ctx-carried Ent transaction. Never call tx.db.Foo() outside this wrapper.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (T, error) { return tx.db.ChargesSearchV1.Query()... })

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements TxCreator via Tx() (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), TxUser[T] via WithTx() (NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self() — all three required for TransactingRepo to rebind or self-start transactions.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: New(Config) calls config.Validate() and errors if Client or Logger is nil before constructing the adapter struct. Never construct adapter directly.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (charges.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{db: config.Client, logger: config.Logger}, nil }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertions
patterns.description: Each implemented interface has a compile-time var _ assertion at file scope.
patterns.example:
var _ charges.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil)
var _ charges.ChargesSearchAdapter = (*adapter)(nil)

patterns.name: Manual Go-side pagination for GroupBy queries
patterns.description: Ent's Paginate() is incompatible with GroupBy; ListCustomersToAdvance fetches all grouped results via Scan() then slices manually in Go.
patterns.example: err := query.GroupBy(fields...).Scan(ctx, &results); start := page.Offset(); pageResults := results[start:end]

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, New constructor, and the adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self for transaction lifecycle. Shared by all query files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never pass a.db directly to a helper — always go through tx *adapter inside TransactingRepo so the Ent client is bound to the current transaction.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GetByIDs, ListCharges, ListCustomersToAdvance using the ChargesSearchV1 Ent view; shows namespace-filter and GroupBy+manual-pagination workaround.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChargesSearchV1 is a read-only Ent view — never write through it. GroupBy results cannot use query.Paginate(); paginate manually in Go.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests against a real Postgres via testutils.InitPostgresDB and migrate.Up(), each using isolated namespaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.Background() at DB setup; prefer t.Context() in new test methods where *testing.T is available.

anti_patterns: Adding a method that reads/writes via a.db without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo — breaks ctx-carried transaction isolation
Writing to ChargesSearchV1 — it is a read-only Ent view backed by underlying charge tables
Using query.Paginate() after GroupBy — Ent does not support this; paginate manually in Go
Constructing the adapter without config.Validate() — nil client panics at query time
Adding business logic (orchestration, state machine calls) to adapter methods — adapters must be pure data-access

decisions.decision: entutils.TransactingRepo wraps every method body rather than at the service call site
decisions.rationale: Charge advancement mixes reads and writes across multiple adapter calls in one transaction; each helper must independently rebind to the ctx-carried tx to avoid partial writes.

decisions.decision: Manual Go-side pagination for ListCustomersToAdvance instead of Ent Paginate
decisions.rationale: Ent's generated Paginate() is incompatible with GROUP BY; total count is computed from the full scan and sliced in Go.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new query method that respects ctx-carried transactions

openmeter/billing/charges/invoiceupdater.purpose: Translates charge-lifecycle patch operations into billing.Service mutations: creates new gathering lines, updates/deletes existing lines on gathering and standard invoices, and resolves charge-ID-based patches by scanning gathering invoices for matching lines.

patterns.name: Patch tagged-union with typed accessor methods
patterns.description: All patch operations are Patch{op, ...} with named constructors (NewCreateLinePatch, NewDeleteLinePatch, NewUpdateLinePatch...) and typed As* accessors that validate op before returning the payload. Never construct Patch{} directly.
patterns.example:
patch := invoiceupdater.NewDeleteLinePatch(lineID, invoiceID)
deletePatch, err := patch.AsDeleteLinePatch()

patterns.name: ApplyPatches fans out by invoice type
patterns.description: Updater.ApplyPatches first resolves charge-ID-based patches to concrete line IDs, then groups patches by invoice ID and dispatches to updateGatheringInvoice or updateMutableStandardInvoice/updateImmutableInvoice based on invoice.Type() and StatusDetails.Immutable.
patterns.example:
if invoice.Type() == billing.InvoiceTypeGathering { return u.updateGatheringInvoice(ctx, ...) }
if !invoice.StatusDetails.Immutable { return u.updateMutableStandardInvoice(ctx, ...) }

patterns.name: Resolve charge-ID-based patches via gathering invoice scan
patterns.description: PatchOpDeleteGatheringLineByChargeID and PatchOpUpdateGatheringLineByChargeID are resolved by listing all gathering invoices for the customer and matching line.ChargeID, converting them to concrete line-level entries before dispatch.
patterns.example:
invoices, _ := u.billingService.ListGatheringInvoices(ctx, ...)
for _, line := range invoice.Lines.OrEmpty() { if _, ok := chargeIDs[*line.ChargeID]; ok { /* add to deletedLines */ } }

patterns.name: Only charge-backed lines mutated
patterns.description: All update/delete patches must target lines with a non-nil ChargeID; ensureLineHasChargeID enforces this before applying any patch. Non-charge lines are left untouched.
patterns.example: if err := ensureLineHasChargeID(line, updatePatch.op); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Empty mutable standard invoice is deleted
patterns.description: After updateMutableStandardInvoice, if NonDeletedLineCount() == 0 and not in Gathering status, DeleteInvoice is called automatically; deletion failures are logged as warnings, not errors.
patterns.example: if updatedInvoice.Lines.NonDeletedLineCount() == 0 { invoice, err := u.billingService.DeleteInvoice(ctx, updatedInvoice.GetInvoiceID()) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines PatchOperation constants, all patch payload structs, the Patch tagged-union, named constructors, As* accessors, and Log() for structured logging.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New patch ops must add a constructor (NewXxxPatch), an As* accessor, a Log() case, and handling in parsePatches. Missing any one causes a panic or silent no-op.

key_file_guides.role: Updater struct, ApplyPatches orchestration, patch resolution and dispatch to billing.Service methods; also LogPatches for dry-run output.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LogPatches suppresses current-period pending-line creates and gathering invoice patches to reduce dry-run noise — do not remove this filtering without understanding the intent.

key_file_guides.role: Pure helpers for reading/writing flat-fee per-unit amount from a billing.GenericInvoiceLine price field.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SetFlatFeePerUnitAmount mutates line.Price in place; callers must ensure the line struct is not shared across goroutines.

anti_patterns: Constructing Patch{} struct literals with op set manually — use named constructors so payload fields are always populated
Adding line mutations directly to the billing adapter from outside invoiceupdater — all charge-line mutations flow through Updater.ApplyPatches
Calling ListGatheringInvoices per individual patch instead of batching — resolution is already batched per customer in resolveGatheringLineDeletesByChargeID
Skipping ensureLineHasChargeID on update/delete patches — non-charge lines must not be mutated
Returning errors for immutable invoice patches without implementing updateImmutableInvoice — immutable paths must be explicitly handled

decisions.decision: Patch operations use a tagged-union struct with private op discriminator rather than an interface
decisions.rationale: Exhaustive dispatch in the parsePatches switch is compile-checked on adding new constants; interface dispatch would silently swallow unknown types.

decisions.decision: Charge-ID-based patches resolved at ApplyPatches time, not at patch creation
decisions.rationale: The charge service caller does not have gathering line IDs at patch generation; lazy resolution inside ApplyPatches keeps the caller API simple.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new patch operation for a new mutation type

openmeter/billing/charges/service.purpose: Orchestration layer for the charges domain: implements charges.Service by routing to flatfee, usagebased, and creditpurchase sub-services, owns the Create/AdvanceCharges/ApplyPatches lifecycle with a two-phase transaction design (creation TX then auto-advance TX), and manages gathering-line creation for invoiceable charges.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all multi-write operations
patterns.description: Create, AdvanceCharges, and HandleCreditPurchaseExternalPaymentStateTransition wrap DB-touching logic in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx) ...) for atomicity. The adapter carries the TxCreator contract.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (charges.Charges, error) { ... })

patterns.name: validateNamespaceLockdown before every write
patterns.description: Create and AdvanceCharges call s.validateNamespaceLockdown(namespace) before any DB work; mandatory for all state-changing service methods.
patterns.example: if err := s.validateNamespaceLockdown(input.Namespace); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Two separate transactions for Create
patterns.description: After the create TX commits, autoAdvanceCreatedCharges identifies customers with newly created credit-only charges and calls AdvanceCharges in a separate TX so creation state persists even if advancement fails.
patterns.example:
result, err := transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx) ...)
return s.autoAdvanceCreatedCharges(ctx, result.charges)

patterns.name: Fan-out to sub-services with index tracking
patterns.description: Create fans out intents to flatFeeService.Create, usageBasedService.Create, creditPurchaseService.Create separately, collecting results with charges.WithIndex[T] to map back to original intent ordering.
patterns.example: charges.WithIndex[charges.Charge]{Index: intent.Index, Value: charges.NewCharge(result.Charge)}

patterns.name: chargesByType classifier for dispatch
patterns.description: chargesByType in helpers.go classifies a charges.Charges slice into flatFees, usageBased, creditPurchase sub-slices for dispatch; panics on unknown charge types — new types must be added here.
patterns.example:
chargesByType, err := chargesByType(inScopeCharges.Items)
for _, charge := range chargesByType.flatFees { ... }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, New constructor, dependency validation; all sub-service and adapter deps injected here, Config.Validate() enforces non-nil for required fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All deps (FlatFeeService, UsageBasedService, CreditPurchaseService, BillingService, FeatureService, MetaAdapter, Adapter) must be non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Implements charges.Service.Create — fans out to sub-services, collects gathering lines, post-commit triggers auto-advance and optional invoice-now for credit purchases.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The two-phase design (TX for creation + separate TX for auto-advance) is intentional; do not merge or crash-recovery loses created state.

key_file_guides.role: Implements AdvanceCharges — wraps in transaction.Run, lists non-final charges, dispatches to flatFeeService.AdvanceCharge or usageBasedService.AdvanceCharge by type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only CreditOnlySettlementMode flat fees are advanced here; non-credit-only flat fees are deliberately skipped.

key_file_guides.role: chargesByType classifier and shared helpers used across create.go, advance.go, patch.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: chargesByType panics on unknown charge types — add new charge types here before adding a new sub-service.

key_file_guides.role: Shared BaseSuite wiring the full charges stack atop billingtest.BaseSuite; all service test suites embed it.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Line engines must be registered on s.BillingService via RegisterLineEngine before tests run — omitting causes silent no-ops during invoice processing.

anti_patterns: Calling sub-service Create/Advance outside a transaction.Run — breaks atomicity across charge types
Merging the create transaction and the auto-advance transaction — creation state lost if advancement panics mid-flight
Adding billing-logic (gathering-line creation) directly to adapter methods — orchestration belongs in service
Calling s.billingService methods without a prior validateNamespaceLockdown — bypasses namespace write protection
Skipping RegisterLineEngine for a new charge type's engine in base_test.go — billing service produces silent no-op lines

decisions.decision: Two separate transactions for Create: creation then auto-advance
decisions.rationale: The balance-worker can retry advancement independently; losing the creation write to an advance panic would be unrecoverable. Separation makes both steps individually durable.

decisions.decision: Service delegates per-type work to flatfee/usagebased/creditpurchase sub-services
decisions.rationale: Each charge type has distinct state machines, realization logic, and handler callbacks; one inline service would be unmanageable and harder to test.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new top-level charges.Service method spanning multiple charge types

openmeter/billing/charges/statemachine.purpose: Generic charge state machine library backed by qmuntal/stateless: Machine[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS] wraps a stateless.StateMachine with external storage bound to in-memory CHARGE, persisting via injected Persistence.UpdateBase after each successful transition. Shared by flatfee, usagebased, and creditpurchase.

patterns.name: ChargeLike constraint for type parameter
patterns.description: CHARGE must implement ChargeLike[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS]: GetChargeID, GetStatus, WithStatus(STATUS) CHARGE (pure copy), GetBase, WithBase(BASE) CHARGE (pure copy). WithStatus/WithBase must be value receivers returning new copies — pointer mutation breaks external storage.
patterns.example:
func (c MyCharge) WithStatus(s MyStatus) MyCharge { c.Status = s; return c }
func (c MyCharge) WithBase(b MyBase) MyCharge { c.Base = b; return c }

patterns.name: FireAndActivate for externally triggered transitions
patterns.description: FireAndActivate checks CanFire, fires the trigger, calls Activate (OnActive callbacks), then persists BASE. Never call m.stateMachine.FireCtx directly — that skips CanFire, Activate, and persistence.
patterns.example: if err := machine.FireAndActivate(ctx, meta.TriggerCollectionCompleted); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: AdvanceUntilStateStable for auto-advancement
patterns.description: Loops FireAndActivate(TriggerNext) until no TriggerNext is available; returns nil when no transitions fired (not an error). Callers must handle the nil return.

patterns.name: Persistence stores BASE, Refetch reloads full CHARGE
patterns.description: UpdateBase persists only the BASE (status-bearing struct) after a transition; Refetch reloads full CHARGE (including edges) when needed after commit.
patterns.example: Persistence[MyCharge, MyBase]{ UpdateBase: func(ctx, base MyBase) (MyBase, error) { return adapter.UpdateCharge(ctx, base) }, Refetch: func(ctx, id meta.ChargeID) (MyCharge, error) { return adapter.GetByID(ctx, id) } }

patterns.name: STATUS Validate() called on every state setter
patterns.description: The external state setter validates incoming status via STATUS.Validate() on every transition. New status types must implement exhaustive Validate(); invalid statuses are caught at runtime.
patterns.example: func (s MyStatus) Validate() error { if !slices.Contains(s.Values(), string(s)) { return models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }; return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Machine[CHARGE,BASE,STATUS], Config, Persistence, ChargeLike, StateMachine interface, New() constructor, FireAndActivate, AdvanceUntilStateStable, RefetchCharge, InvoicePatches/DrainInvoicePatches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FireAndActivate calls Activate after Fire to trigger OnActive callbacks before persisting; if Activate errors, persistence is skipped — in-memory charge may have advanced status but no DB write occurred.

key_file_guides.role: Reference ChargeLike implementations (fakeCharge/fakeBase/fakeStatus) and test patterns for FireAndActivate, AdvanceUntilStateStable, RefetchCharge.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use t.Context(); use fakeCharge as the canonical template when implementing ChargeLike for a new charge type.

anti_patterns: Implementing WithStatus/WithBase as pointer receivers that mutate in place — must return new value copies because Machine.Charge is updated by assignment
Calling m.stateMachine.FireCtx directly instead of FireAndActivate — skips CanFire guard, Activate, and persistence
Omitting OnActive callbacks when a state requires side effects on entry — activation runs between Fire and Persist
Returning an invalid STATUS from a transition action — the external state setter Validate() catches it at runtime
Ignoring the nil return from AdvanceUntilStateStable — nil means no transitions fired, not an error

decisions.decision: Generic Machine[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS] instead of per-type state machines
decisions.rationale: Three charge types share identical transition mechanics (fire, activate, persist-base, refetch); generics eliminate duplication while keeping type safety without reflection.
Charge status is stored in the domain struct, not inside the stateless library; keeps the machine stateless and allows refetch without re-creating it.
Base fields (status, schedule) are what must be durable after a transition; realizations and edges load on demand via Refetch, keeping writes minimal.

code_examples.scenario: Configure and drive a charge state machine for a new charge type

openmeter/billing/charges/testutils.purpose: Shared test infrastructure for the charges domain: provides MockHandlers (no-op flatfee.Handler, creditpurchase.Handler, usagebased.Handler with deterministic ledger references) and a NewServices factory that wires the full charges stack from raw dependencies, mirroring production wiring without importing app/common.

patterns.name: MockHandlers as zero-side-effect handler implementations
patterns.description: mockFlatFeeHandler, mockCreditPurchaseHandler, mockUsageBasedHandler implement all handler methods. Ledger callbacks return a fresh ULID-based ledgertransaction.GroupReference per invocation so tests can distinguish which callback ran.
patterns.example:
handlers := testutils.NewMockHandlers()
flatFeeService, _ := flatfeeservice.New(flatfeeservice.Config{Handler: handlers.FlatFee, ...})

patterns.name: NewServices wires the full charges stack
patterns.description: NewServices(t, Config) constructs meta adapter, locker, lineage adapter/service, each charge-type adapter/service, line engines, and the top-level charges.Service in one call; also calls BillingService.RegisterLineEngine for flatfee and usagebased.
patterns.example: svcs, err := testutils.NewServices(t, testutils.Config{ Client: dbClient, BillingService: billingSvc, FlatFeeHandler: handlers.FlatFee, ... })

patterns.name: Config.Validate() guards all required dependencies
patterns.description: Config.Validate() returns joined errors for any nil required field (Client, BillingService, FeatureService, StreamingConnector, all three handlers). RecognizerService defaults to recognizer.NoopService{} if nil.
patterns.example:
if config.RecognizerService == nil { config.RecognizerService = recognizer.NoopService{} }
if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

key_file_guides.role: Mock implementations of flatfee.Handler, creditpurchase.Handler, usagebased.Handler with compile-time interface assertions and newMockLedgerTransactionGroupReference() returning fresh ULIDs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When a new method is added to any Handler interface, add a corresponding no-op here or the mock stops compiling. Each callback returns a fresh ULID — tests verifying specific IDs must capture the return value.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate(), Services struct, and NewServices factory constructing the full charges sub-service tree for tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewServices registers flatfee, usagebased, and creditpurchase line engines on BillingService. Adding a new charge type requires adding its adapter, service, and line engine registration here.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from testutils — creates import cycles; build all dependencies from package constructors directly
Adding stateful side effects to mock handlers without a Reset() method — causes test pollution across suite cases
Using NewServices in production wiring (app/common) — this factory is test-only with mock handlers and no real ledger integration
Relying on a fixed ledger transaction group ID — mock callbacks return fresh ULIDs each invocation

decisions.decision: MockHandlers returns a new ULID on every ledger callback instead of a fixed constant
decisions.rationale: Tests verifying ledger transaction group IDs need unique, distinguishable IDs per callback; a fixed constant would prevent differentiating which callback ran or how many times.

decisions.decision: NewServices wires all sub-services rather than requiring callers to compose them
decisions.rationale: The charges stack has a deep dependency tree; centralizing setup reduces test boilerplate and ensures consistent wiring across integration tests.

code_examples.scenario: Setting up the full charges stack in an integration test
code_examples.code:
handlers := testutils.NewMockHandlers()
svcs, err := testutils.NewServices(t, testutils.Config{
    Client:                dbClient,
    BillingService:        billingSvc,
    FeatureService:        featureSvc,
    StreamingConnector:    mockStreamingConnector,
    FlatFeeHandler:        handlers.FlatFee,
    CreditPurchaseHandler: handlers.CreditPurchase,
    UsageBasedHandler:     handlers.UsageBased,
})
require.NoError(t, err)
// svcs.ChargesService, svcs.FlatFeeService, svcs.UsageBasedService, svcs.CreditPurchaseService
